blockingIssues.description: `/ai-review rerun` and `/ai-review focus` generate idempotency keys with `time.Now().UnixNano()`, so command retries/replays can create duplicate runs instead of at-most-once command effects keyed to the note delivery.

Note hook persistence and command execution are not atomic; if command execution fails after the hook event insert, a retried delivery is deduplicated and the rerun/ignore/resolve/focus side effect is permanently lost.

The supposed tree-state-finalization commit bundled substantial new behavior (command processor, note routing, and writer changes) instead of only legalizing existing residue, so it violated its own feature contract.

Degradation summary rendering now depends on `review_runs.error_detail`, but the degradation path never persists that summary there, so degraded runs fall back to a generic summary instead of the required explicit partial-coverage note.
The degradation branch does not persist the skipped-file/why summary where the writer can read it, so VAL-BETA-018 is not satisfied end-to-end.
The requested VAL-PERF-001 benchmark corpus/report is still missing from the implementation and worker handoff.
Runtime provider selection is hard-coded to `default`/`secondary` and never uses effective project/group policy, so provider-route-per-project/group is not implemented end-to-end.
Directory-scoped `REVIEW.md` is loaded as one global winner for the run instead of applying the nearest directory review per changed file.
`.gitlab/ai-review.yaml` parsing discards validation warnings and silently accepts unknown fields, so invalid config is not surfaced as required.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how workers should handle features whose intended behavior is already present in dirty branch state or prior commits: require them to distinguish between true implementation work and tree-state finalization/validation-unblock commits, and to verify end-to-end runtime behavior rather than only metadata/tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `beta-command-webhooks` and `beta-provider-routing-rate-limits` both had review feedback that workers validated partial or pre-existing logic without proving the runtime contract end-to-end; the tree-finalization feature also shipped behavioral work beyond its scope.

rejectedObservations.observation: The beta-large-mr-degradation review noted that the go-reliability skill procedure itself was followed successfully.
